rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component coupling of 1,3-dicarbonyls (e.g., dibenzoylmethane) with styrenes and water to give β-functionalized γ-acyloxy ketones via radical-polar crossover and an oxocarbenium intermediate
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the deprotonated 1,3-dicarbonyl enolate to a C-centered radical that adds to styrene; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical to a benzylic cation. Cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2, providing base and the oxygen nucleophile.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic radical adduct after styrene addition (III)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces H2O to OH- + H2; OH- deprotonates the 1,3-dicarbonyl, the resulting enolate is oxidized at the anode to a C-radical that adds to styrene; the benzylic radical is oxidized again at the anode to a benzylic cation, which is trapped intramolecularly by a carbonyl oxygen to give an oxocarbenium that is opened by water/OH- to deliver the γ-acyloxy ketone.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO-adduct of the 1,3-dicarbonyl radical isolated in 55%; product 3aa formed only in trace amounts, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: BHT-adduct 1a'' detected by HRMS; product 3aa not observed, supporting radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: Product formed with 99% deuterium incorporation, confirming moisture (H2O) is the proton/O-H source

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporation into the benzoate oxygen of product detected by HRMS (m/z 369.1369, calcd 369.1353 for C23H20-16O2-18O1Na), confirming water as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: ethyl benzoylacetate 1a1 + α-methylstyrene 2p (H2O)
probe_reagents.observation: γ-butyrolactone d1 (C18H17O3, [M+H]+ 281.1172) detected by NMR/HRMS, consistent with OH- opening of the proposed oxocarbenium

probe_reagents.name: MeOH (in place of H2O) with 1a + 2p
probe_reagents.observation: Methyl ether adduct d2 (C25H25O3, [M+H]+ 372.1784) detected by HRMS, confirming nucleophilic trapping of the oxocarbenium intermediate

probe_reagents.name: divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Case 1 gave 3aa in 60%; Case 2 gave no product, indicating both anodic and cathodic processes are required

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: CV of 1a and 2a in CH3CN:H2O with Bu4PF6 used to establish oxidation potentials and order of electron transfer

notes: An alternative pathway shown in the proposed cycle leads to allylic/olefinic product 5aa via HAT/elimination from intermediate III' (formed when the dicarbonyl radical adds to a terminal alkene 4a instead of styrene), suggesting competing oxocarbenium ring-closure (V') vs β-H elimination depending on substrate. The favored pathway for styrenes goes through benzylic cation IV → cyclic oxocarbenium V → lactol VI → ring-opened benzoate VII → product 3aa.
